frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
Meena: Main bas practical baat kar rahi hoon, aajkal zamaana bahut kharab hai. Ladki ko thoda sambhal ke rehna chahiye, raat ko akeli ghoomne ya kahin bhi aane-jaane se bachna chahiye.
Husband: Haan, sahi keh rahi ho.
Meena: Galti kisi ki bhi ho, nuksan toh ladki ka hi hota hai, isliye apni safety ka dhyan usko khud hi rakhna padta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's safety as their personal responsibility and normalizes restricting women's movement instead of addressing male violence. It uses protective language like 'zamaana kharab hai' and 'sambhal ke rehna chahiye' to justify victim-blaming and behavioral control.
original_frame: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 54. Married for 28 years. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your son Karan who is 29 just got married to a girl named Ritu who is from Delhi. Ritu is a working professional and seems confident and modern. At the wedding Ritu wore sindoor and the mangalsutra but since coming to your house she has only worn the mangalsutra Ñ no sindoor on most days. You have not said anything to Ritu. But you are sitting with your husband at night after Ritu and Karan have gone to their room. You are telling your husband what you have observed and what it means and what you think needs to be said and by whom.
